steps.title: Distribute the factor
steps.content:
To find an equivalent expression, apply the outside factor $5$ to each term inside the parentheses. This keeps the value of the expression the same for every value of $x$:
$$5(x^2+7)=5\cdot x^2+5\cdot 7$$

steps.title: Simplify the products
steps.content:
Now multiply each term from the distribution:
$$5\cdot x^2+5\cdot 7=5x^2+35$$
The term $5x^2$ comes from multiplying $5$ by $x^2$, and $35$ comes from multiplying $5$ by $7$. The equivalent expression is $5x^2+35$. The answer is A.
